judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is more complete: it independently catches the nightly_export breakage (expectation b), which Report 1 misses entirely, while both correctly identify the tenant-isolation leak and correctly avoid over-flagging the SQL string as exploitable.

report_1.evidence: There is **no `tenant_id = :tenant_id` predicate**, even though the function receives `tenant_id` and the route resolves it via `Depends(current_tenant_id)` (`backend/app/api.py:19-22`) — the parameter is accepted and then silently ignored. Any authenticated user of any clinic who calls `GET /appointments/search?q=...&status=booked` gets matching appointments from **every tenant in the database**

report_2.evidence: There is no `tenant_id` predicate at all — the `tenant_id` parameter is accepted by the function and then never used. Any authenticated user of any clinic can full-text-search the appointment **notes** (free-text clinical content — PHI), provider names, and schedules of **every other tenant** in the system.

The PR changed `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` to `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id, filters)` with `filters` as a **required** positional parameter, but `nightly_export` in `exports.py` (untouched by the PR) still calls it with two arguments: `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id).all()`. Every run of the nightly per-tenant CSV export will raise `TypeError: build_appointment_query() missing 1 required positional argument: 'filters'`

report_1.evidence: Today the blast radius is limited because FastAPI validates `status` against the closed `AppointmentStatus` enum (`backend/app/schemas.py`: only `booked`/`cancelled`/`completed`), so an attacker cannot currently inject arbitrary SQL through this route.

report_2.evidence: the f-string interpolation of `status.value` into the SQL (`appointments.py:16`) looks like SQL injection, but it is not exploitable as written — `status` is typed `AppointmentStatus` (a `str` Enum limited to `booked`/`cancelled`/`completed` in `backend/app/schemas.py`), so FastAPI rejects any other value with a 422 before the handler runs.
